Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Saturday, January 4th, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a report of a motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I met the registered owner, who reported that his vehicle was parked in the driveway at 21:30 hours on 01/03/2025. He further reported that he left his residence with his friends in his friend's vehicle and returned to his residence on the morning of 01/04/2025. He noticed his vehicle was taken from his driveway. The registered owner reported there was nothing valuable in the car and was uncertain if the spare keys were in the vehicle. Both New Jersey plates are affixed to the vehicle.      Communications advised me that the vehicle was located in Clarkstown, NY, and was involved in a motor vehicle hit-and-run accident. The registered owner was also advised of the vehicle's location. I observed no cameras in the area.      The victim did not want the vehicle processed or to sign complaints if the suspect(s) are identified.     A New Jersey Victim Notification form was completed and provided to the registered owner.     An ATTF entry was completed    A motor vehicle checklist was completed and submitted.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Friday January 10, 2025, I was assigned as the Hackensack High School SRO. I was advised that a student wanted to report a crime and was currently in the main office. Upon arriving to the main office I met the victim, A.R.       As I began to speak with the victim she immediately began to cry. She stated that she was inappropriately touched by the suspect who is also student. The suspect was identified as Manuel Martinez Perez. The victim stated that on Tuesday January 7, 2025  she was in her 9th period class ESL 2 in room 276. She shares this class with the suspect and his cousin Esmeralda Perez Martinez who is a potential witness.       The victim stated during class the suspect gave her a hug. As he was hugging her he dropped his hand down and grabbed her breast over her clothing. She immediately became angry and told him not to touch her. She then advised Esmeralda Perez Martinez that she was touched inappropriately by the suspect. The victim stated that she reported this incident to her her teacher Mrs. Magdalene Symmonds, but she did not understand her due to a language barrier.  The victim reported that this was not the first time she was inappropriately touched by the suspect. She reported that the prior week a similar incident occurred. I notified assistant Principal Celso King of this report.      I spoke to Esmeralda Perez Martinez who may have witness the incident. Perez Martinez stated that she sits in a group with the victim and the suspect. The day of the incident she did not observe the suspect touch the victim because she was busy doing her class work. The witness did confirm that the victim mentioned to her that she was inappropriately touched by the suspect.       The victim's mother, Raquel Lelio, was notified of the incident and responded to the school. She advised me that she was not sure if she wanted to sign criminal complaints against the suspect. She wants to speak with her daughter and husband before making a decision.       Det. E. Reyes was advised of the incident and will follow up with the complainant at a later time.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect(s): Unknown.     I responded to the listed location, 90 Prospect Avenue, on a report of a burglary to auto. Upon arrival, I was met by the victim, Kelly Nurnberg-Darzi, who stated on the morning of 01/10/25 she responded to her grey 2006 Honda Pilot and observed all of her work equipment to be missing. The victim advised me she specialized in opening safes and stores several of her high-value tools in the rear of the listed vehicle. The victim stated the listed vehicle was parked in assigned space #20 and did not move it from the assigned space. The victim stated she last recalled observing her tools in the listed vehicle on 01/06/25 and had not returned to the vehicle since. The following items were taken from the listed vehicle:2 Boroscope - $5,000.00 USD8 Redundant Locks - $2,000.00 USD5 Drills - $5,000.00 USD1 large box of drill bits - $1,200.00 USD1 tool bag with several tools - $2,000.00 USD6 Electronic locks - $1,320.00 USD4 FAS locks - $480.00 Total Value - $17,000.00 USD     While on scene, I observed several cameras in the parking garage of 90 Prospect Avenue. I further observed the listed vehicle to have a broken rear passenger side window which gives direct access to the vehicles trunk. The victim stated the damage occurred on 01/04/25 while her son was parking the vehicle. The victim further stated she did not have any time to repair the broken window and remove the tools from the vehicle prior to them being taken.      The victim advised me she believes the suspect may be her husband Uri Darzi whom she is currently in a civil lawsuit with. Sgt. Peralta responded assist.A victim notification form was completed and a copy was given to the victim.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Monday, January 13th, 2025, at approximately 12:07 hours, P.O. Alvarez and I responded to the listed location on a dispute report. Before our arrival, headquarters had advised us that the male suspect, described as wearing a grey hoodie and sweatpants, had fled southbound on First Street. Upon arrival, we met with Maria Romero Peralta, the mother of the juvenile victim, A.R.      She reported that the described suspect, later identified as D.T., had punched her in the left eye and her right breast. With the mother's permission and in her presence, we spoke to the victim, a juvenile male, A.R., who reported that the suspect, D.T., had stolen a red electronic moped bike and had dropped it off at his house. He further reported that the suspect asked the victim if he could drop off the stolen moped for a couple of days. The victim also reported giving the stolen moped bike to another friend since he did not know how to operate the bike. A.R. later noted that the suspect, D.T., recovered the stolen moped bike from the said friend they have in common and had the bike confiscated byLaw Enforcement in another jurisdiction.      Today, the victim reported that the suspect arrived at his residence demanding $400.00 for the moped because it had been confiscated. He further reported that the suspect arrived with three (3) other friends: two Hispanic males, unknown names, dates of birth, or addresses, and a Hispanic female, blonde hair and blue jacket, operating a Toyota Rav 4 with an unknown NY tag. He also reported that a fight ensued between him and the suspect, and the three (3) witnesses stood by.      The victim also reported that his mother, Maria Romero Peralta, heard the commotion and broke up the fight, retreating with the victim and herself back into the residence. A.R. reported that the front entrance door closed and was being held by his mother when the suspect breached the door with physical force and continued to assault the victim while demanding $400.00 for the red moped electric bike.  I then requested a supervisor, and Sgt. Williams responded to assist.     Maria Romero Peralta reported that while attempting to break up the fight, she was punched in the left eye and right breast by the suspect, D.T. She also reported that the suspect and the three (3) witnesses fled the scene after she threatened to call the police. Maria Romero Peralta, along with A.R. declined medical attention at the scene. The mother and the victim wanted to provide statements to detectives and were transported to headquarters by P.O. Alvarez as I followed.      At headquarters, Detectives Mora and Garrett responded to report room 2 and met with the victim and his mother. Before providing statements to the detectives, Maria Romero Peralta began to have complaints of pain in her left eye and right breast. I observed her left eye to be swollen and red. Maria Romero Peralta then requested to be transported to the hospital for evaluation and treatment. I requested an ambulance, and Hackensack University Medical Center BLS #136 arrived. The mother, now a patient, was transported to the HUMC along with A.R. for treatment. The patient reported that she would respond to headquarters at a later time to provide statements to the detectives. I contacted DCP&P Christine #1718 and advised of the incident. She reported that the incident would only be documented. I completed a victim notification form and provided a copy to the victim.It should be noted that the victim, A.R., reported that he had dropped out of school as of today; therefore, a handle with care was not completed. See Detectives report for additional information.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Tuesday, January 14th, 2025, P.O. Conlon. P.O. Voorhis, and I responded to 450 Hackensack Avenue for a report of a trespasser on the premises who had shoplifted in the past and has also been aggressive with staff in the past.    Upon my arrival, I observed the arrestee enter a white Volkswagon (NJ-Y82VDS) that was leaving the parking lot. The security officers at Home Depot also pointed out the arrestee as he entered the car. I conducted a motor vehicle stop with the vehicle at the traffic light exiting the parking lot. As I approached the vehicle, the arrestee exited the vehicle and started running West on Hackensack Avenue. P.O. Conlon, P.O. Voorhis, and I began running after the arrestee and announcing ourselves as Hackensack Police. When the arrestee approached the other side of Hackensack Avenue, he stopped, and P.O. Conlon apprehended him. The arrestee was flailing his arms while I was attempting to place him in handcuffs. The arrestee was then put in handcuffs with his hands behind his back. The arrestee was then searched, which yielded negative for contraband.   The Loss Prevention Officers stated that the arrestee entered the front door of Home Depot with a black ski covering his face. The loss prevention officers noticed the arrestee from previous shoplifting. The arrestee then concealed several power tools totaling $1,284.00 and placed them in a shopping cart. The arrestee then went past all points of sale and tried to exit the store. Prior to exiting the store, the loss prevention officers approached him and tried to stop him from leaving. The arrestee then pushed the loss prevention officers and fled the store on foot. The loss prevention officer recovered the items. The arrestee then entered the listed vehicle and fled the parking lot for the exit of the mall. A photo copy of the receipt was placed into the BCI mailbox located at the front at the front desk.  The items that were recovered: M18 Jobsite Radio priced at $189.00Two 20V Atomic BL Combo Kit priced at $149.00 eachM18 Fuel Jig Saw priced at $199.00MKE M12 Packout Flood Light priced at $149.00M18 Fuel 10 Miter Saw priced at $449.00     Totaling at $1,284.00.The arrestee was placed in radio car 114 and transported to police headquarters for processing. The vehicle was checked before and after the transport for contraband which yielded negative   The arrestee was processed at police headquarters by P.O. Caero. Complaint W-2025-000016 was authored by Lt. Swetits against the arrestee and was signed for 2C:29-2a(1), 2C:29-2a(2), and two counts of 2C:15-1A(1).  The arrestee also had multiple outstanding warrants from various jurisdictions.The arrestee was remanded to the Bergen County Jail.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On 01/15/2025 I responded to 244 First Street on a reported motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival I met with the driver of the stolen vehicle, Jeffrey Mejiamorquecho. He reported he parked his motor vehicle in front of 244 First Street facing north at around 24:00 hours. He further reported his mother left his residence at 05:00 hours on the listed date and noticed the vehicle was gone. The driver also reported he received a notification from EZ Pass with a charge from the George Washington Bridge toll at 04:13 hours on the listed date. The driver then walked out with me to where his vehicle was parked and observed broken glass which he suspected to be from his rear passenger side window. The driver advised me the vehicle had 1 plate in the rear and was worth approximately $50,000.00 as the vehicle was fairly new. He further reported he attempted to track the vehicle as it contains tracking equipment but was unsuccessful as it was not connecting. He believes it was disconnected somehow after the vehicle was stolen.240 First Street has cameras in the general direction of where the vehicle was parked. It is unknown at this time if they captured the incident occur.Insurance Information: Company: Geico (369)Policy Number: 6183163036The driver was provided with a completed New Jersey Victim Notification Form.The driver wished to have the vehicle processed if located and a Hackensack Police Consent to Search from was completed.The vehicle was entered into NCIC as stolen by Communications.The vehicle was entered into ATTF as stolen.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Thursday, January 16, 2025 at approximately 1934 hours, Sgt. Peralta and I responded to 30 Second Street, Apt. L1 on report of a residential burglary. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who reported that she left her apartment at approximately 1200 hours on this date, before returning several hours later, at approximately 1800 hours. The victim further reported that upon returning to her apartment, she observed the exterior door knob to be damaged (loose), as if someone attempted to break in. The victim stated that upon entering the apartment, the interior did not appear to be ransacked, nor did anything appear out of place at this time.      The scene was unable to processed due to the victim contaminating evidence while entering her apartment. The cost of repair for the damaged door knob was approximately $35.00. A Victim Notification was completed and the victim was provided with a copy upon our departure. There were no known suspects at the time this report was taken. Det./Sgt. Tripodi was notified.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect 1: Hispanic male, approximately early 30' s in age, approximately 5'10" in height, approximately 180 in weight, wearing a tan hooded sweater, brown "NY" Yankees logo hat, black sweatpants and white sneakers.Suspect 2: Hispanic female, approximately mid 20's in age, approximately 5'5" in height, approximately 140 in weight, wearing a black jacket, grey hooded sweater, tan pants and cream colored shoes. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Thursday, 01/23/2025 I was notified by D/Sgt. Dominguez of a possible sexual assault involving a juvenile. I was advised that 10 y/o S.S. disclosed to a school teacher that her mothers boyfriend has been touching her while her mother was sleeping. This reportedly has occurred on multiple occasions. No further information was available at this time. S.S. is a student at Nelly K. Parker school, who already notified DCP&P as per protocol.    I contacted DCP&P social worker Jennifer Diaz who was assigned to the initial investigation. Ms. Diaz advised me that she was currently enroute to Nelly K. Parker to meet with S.S., and school staff. She advised me that she already contacted BCPO/SVU. I advised her that I will meet her at the school. Upon arrival I met with Ms. Diaz, who advised me that S.S. was set to be picked up from school by another unknown adult possibly the suspect. Due to the circumstances it was determined that S.S. will be transported to BCPO (100 Eisenhower Drive, Paramus NJ) to undergo a forensic interview with SVU. Contact was made to S.S. mother, Ibys Sandoval, who was advised that a police investigation is underway and her presence is required. Ms. Sandoval expressed that she has transportation issues, therefore this agency arranged transport for her.  Det. Reyes responded to pick up Ms. Sandoval at her residence (176 Berdan Place, Hackensack NJ). Ms. Sandoval was transported to Nelly K. Parker to meet up with her daughter, and ultimately transported to BCPO for the interview.   Contact information was limited at the time of this report (See Det. Reyes Supplemental report for further details)

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On January 25, 2025, at approximately 13:55 hours, P.O. P. Lopez, P.O. G. Lara-Nunez, P.O. Mattalian, Sgt. Competiello, and I responded to 60 Kansas apartment 8F for a possible burglary in progress. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who reported she left her apartment at approximately 11:30 hours. The victim reported approximately 14:00 hours, she returned to the apartment to find the front door slightly ajar.     We did a check of the interior of the apartment which yielded negative results for any suspects inside. The victim reported she is 100% sure the door was locked because she checked multiple times before leaving. The victim reported she was missing about $2,000 in miscellaneous jewelry. She described the jewelry to be earrings and gold necklaces.The victim reported she had the same incident occur in 2023. See 23-091001 for more. The victim reported she observed a light skin Hispanic male driving a black Lexus leaving the apartment complex as she pulled in. The victim reported she recalls the same Lexus and same male in the area after the previous incident.Sgt. Competiello contacted detectives. Detective DeBonis arrived on scene.The victim requested for the apartment to be processed. Sgt. Competiello contacted Bergen County Sheriff's Office. Detective LaSala #1866 arrived on scene. See BCSO case #2025-0052 for more .I observed no signs of forced entry. The New Jersey victim notification form was filled out and a copy was provided to the victim for her records.The crime scene was secured at 14:15 hours.Crime Scene Log:14:31: Det. LaSala Enters14:38: Det. LaSala Exits14:48: Det. Lasala Enters14:49: Det. Debonis Enters15:01: Det. LaSala Exits.15:04: Det. LaSala Enters.Crime Scene Terminated: 15:08

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect description: Possibly male, approximately 5'7'' in height, with a slim build. On 1/26/25 P.O. Papanikolaou and I responded to 206 Stanley Place on a reported theft from auto. Upon inspecting her vehicle nothing appeared to be stolen and no damage was observed. A New Jersey victim notification form was completed. The victim did not request to have the vehicle processed. At this time the victim reported if the suspect could be identified she would pursue a criminal case.  The victim was advised to save the video surveillance of the incident. Sgt. Competiello was advised.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx

She was further advised on how to obtain a copy of this report.  Detective Garrett was notified.Vehicle Insurance: GeicoCode :365Policy Number: 6164-87-88-42

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On January 31, 2025, at approximately 1200 hours the victim responded to Hackensack Police Department Headquarters for a report of a burglary to his residence that occurred in the past. I met with the victim who reported that last Friday, January 24, 2025 at approximately 1200 hours he left his residence with his wife and son for the day. He reported that upon his return to his residence at approximately 1500 hours, he observed the front door (allows entry into the apartment) wide open.  He further reported that he believes he locked the door before he left but did not observe any damage to his door. He reported that he went inside his residence to check if anyone was in his apartment with negative results. The victim reported that the following day, Saturday, January 25, 2025, he looked for his beige folder that contained copies and originals of the following items: passport, social security cards, and licenses of his wife, Arelis Bonilla, and his sons, Eduardo M. Sobrino and Edwin D. Sobrino-Bonilla.       The victim reported that he last saw his beige folder with those documents in his living room approximately one month ago. The victim was advised to contact the social security administration, DMV, and credit companies to advise them of the above. He was also advised on how to obtain new copies of his social security card, license, and passport. The victim was advised to report any fraudulent activity he may see.       A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and a copy was provided to the victim. He was advised to contact Hackensack Police Department should another incident like this arise.       The victim was not offered B.C.I. to respond to the residence due to the apartment being contaminated for approximately a week. Det. Lt. Weber was notified.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Unknown Suspect Descriptions:Unknown Suspect 1: Tall white male, crew cut, no clothing descriptionUnknown Suspects 2-5: 4 males, unknown race, wearing dark colored helmets and dark clothes with padding.         While in the area, P.O. Maceri and I were flagged down by the victim. Upon speaking with him, he advised that he had been robbed. Sgt. Farhi responded to assist.          The victim stated that on 02/02/2025 between 17:25 and 18:00 hours, he was walking north on Main Street, near the area of Main Street and Court Street with two acquaintances. He further stated that while walking in that area, a verbal dispute took place between the three of them. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Monday, February 3, 2025 at approximately 1640 hours, Sgt. Farhi and I responded to 400 Hackensack Avenue (Marc Jacobs) on a reported robbery. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who reported that three suspects (1 male, 2 females) entered the establishment at approximately 1600 hours. The suspects reportedly exited and reentered the store multiples time prior to committing the offense, with the last entry being made only by the male suspect.     The victim further reported that the suspect grabbed three small purses from the display shelf before he began to walk toward the exit. At that time, the victim stated that he attempted to stop the suspect, who immediately shoved him into a store fixture. The suspect reportedly proceeded to run out of the store toward the nearest mall exit, where he entered a black vehicle and fled in an unknown direction. The victim stated that he was able to recover one of the purses during the incident.     The victim advised that he sustained minor injuries to his left arm as result of the incident, in which he refused any medical attention. The victim wished to provide a recorded statement to detectives. He was transported to police headquarters where he was turned over to Det. Dogali. The total loss suffered by the establishment for the two purses was approximately $650.00. The store manager (witness) wished to have complaints signed against the suspects if they were able to be identified.     Suspect 1: Black male, slim build, 5'8", black beanie, black puffer, dark pants, white/blue sneakers     Suspect 2: Black female, heavy build, 5'8", short hair, black puffer, black leggings, black pocketbook     Suspect 3: Black female, heavy build, 5'7", long cornrows, gray puffer, light blue jeans

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: I responded to the listed location on a reported motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I spoke with the complainant. She stated that on 02/04/2025 at 11:29 hours, she received a text from her boss, asking her to check on one of their U-Haul vehicles. She further stated that upon searching the parking lot, she could not locate the vehicle. The complainant reported that the vehicle had been rented out, so she contacted the contract holder to ensure the vehicle was dropped off. Upon verifying such and checking camera footage, the vehicle is seen on camera being dropped off by the customer. He is then approached by the suspect, where a brief verbal exchange is had. The customer is then seen leaving the area. The suspect is then seen retrieving keys from a lock box, before entering the van and fleeing the area.     I then spoke with the customer. He stated that he had dropped the vehicle off at the listed location at approximately 18:40 hours. He further stated that upon dropping it off, he was greeted by the suspect, who acted as an employee of the business. The customer reported that after a brief discussion, he was wary of the suspect and secured the keys in the lock box himself. He further stated that he then left the area.       A check in house of our tow list was conducted, with the vehicle not having been reported as towed or repossessed. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Wednesday, February 5, 2025 at approximately 0300 hours,  P.O Dominguez and I responded to Hackensack Pharmacy (441 Passaic Street) on a reported activated burglar alarm.   Upon my arrival, I observed the main entrance glass door located on the north side of the business to be shattered.  I also observed a master lock and a metal pin on the ground near the door that appeared to had been cut off the steel roller gate located in front of the glass door. I also observed a white prescription pill bottle on the ground near the master lock.  I advised police communications of my findings and P.O A. Lopez , P.O N. Marza and P.O Mattalian arrived to assist.  A perimeter of the business was immediately established.  P.O Dominguez, P.O N. Marza and I made entry into the business and completed an interior check.  The interior check yielded negative results for any person(s) in the business.  While completing the interior check, I observed two gray large paver stones on the floor near the front glass door.  It appears the two stones were used to break the glass to the front door to gain access.  I observed several white prescription pill bottles on the floor as well as several pairs of eye glasses.  Upon further inspection, I observed a white wooden cabinet located behind the service counter that appeared to have been broken into which contained several white prescription pill bottles similar to the prescription pill bottle located outside on the ground in front of the business.  P.O Dominguez made notification to the tour commander. At approximately 0445 hours, Det. Scarpa and the business owner, Mr. Bodawala, arrived and were updated.  Mr. Bodawala consented to the business to be processed and signed a Consent to Search form.  Mr. Bodawala showed video footage of the incident to Det. Scarpa and e-mailed the footage to Det. Scarpa.  See Det. Scarpa's Supplemental report for further. At 0315 hours, the scene was preserved and a crime log had commenced.-0448 hours, Det. N. LaSala (BCSD-CSU) arrived to process the scene, BCSD case # 2025-0088.-0528 hours, Det. N. LaSala completed processing the scene. The scene was turned over to Mr. Bodawala. Mr. Bodawala advised it is unknown what proceeds/value were taken at this time. He advised once he and his employee conduct and complete an inventory, he will compile a list and forward it to Det. Scarpa. Case turned over to Det. Scarpa.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On February 18, 2025, I was advised by Sgt. Tripodi that a female, later identified as Yamini Verma, a victim of sexual assault was going to be seen at Mt. Sinai hospital in New York City.  At approximately 17:00 hours, I received a call from NYPD SVU Detective Woo that Ms. Verma was confirmed a patient at Mt. Sinai Emergency Room and was undergoing the SART Examination.    Det. Tanucilli and I responded to Mt. Sinai emergency room to meet with the victim.  Upon our arrival the RN Rachel stated that the victim was still undergoing the SART examination.  Once the examination was completed Det. Tanucilli and I entered the room and spoke with the victim (BWC was activated).    The victim told us that she was sexually assaulted by the suspect Akashpreet Riar on February 17, 2025.  The victim stated that the suspect is a teacher's assistant at the New York College of Podiatric Medicine, where she attends.  She stated that she was in communication with the suspect because she was looking for a tutor and he offered to help her.  The suspect picked up the victim in a blue Honda (unknown registration) from her residence in New York City at approximately 1930 hours.  The victim and suspect arrived at the suspect's apartment (240 Prospect Avenue, unknown apartment).  The victim and suspect were studying for approximately 1 hours, before both parties started to have a few drinks of alcohol.  The victim stated that the suspect started to approach her and speak in a sexual manner.  The victim stated that she refused his advancements and told him that she is not interested.  The victim stated that the suspect started to grope her breasts, buttocks and thighs while trying to kiss her neck.  The victim felt scared and again tried to push off the suspect.  The victim stated that she went to the bathroom and when she finished and unlocked the door, the suspect forced his way into the bathroom.  She stated that the suspect forced her to pull down her pants and shoved his hand down the front of her pants.  She stated that her head was pushed into the sink and the suspect began to digitally penetrate the victim's vagina.  The victim stated that she told the suspect to stop but felt like she had no control over the situation.  The victim stated that she was then picked up and placed into the bathtub where the suspect inserted his penis into her vagina against her will.  The victim said she was raped for approximately 2 minutes and the suspect ejaculated next to the victim.    The victim stated she was in a daze of confusion and fear.  She stated that the suspect called an UBER for her and she took the ride share back to NYC.  The victim stated that she was in disbelief and called her Dean of the college, Dr. Chusid in the morning (2/18/2025).  The victim stated that she told her dean that she was sexually assaulted, and he then recommended she seek medical attention the the nearest hospital.  At the emergency room,  evidence was collected which included the victim's bra (item #1), shirt (item #2), jeans (item #3) and the SART collection kit (item #4).  The victim stated that she wished to continue with a police investigation.  At that point  I contacted the Bergen County Prosecutor's office and spoke to Det. Chris Miranda.   He instructed me to see if the victim is available to respond to BCPO Headquarters in Paramus for an interview on Wednesday, February  19, 2025 at 1700 hours.  The victim agreed.  The evidence was turned over to Det. Tanucilli . Back at police headquarters, an evidence report was generated and the evidence was secured in temp. evidence locker #3 and the temp. evidence refrigerator.    On February 19, 2025, I responded to the Bergen County Prosecutor's Office to meet with the Special Victim's Unit detectives and the victim.  The victim spoke with Detectives Miranda and Dawson.  After meeting with the BCPO SVU detectives the victim stated that she wanted to apply for a SASPA Protection Order.  She was advised to respond to Hackensack Police Headquarters during business hours to obtain a copy of the police report and then go the Bergen County Superior Court and apply in person for the protection order.  The victim stated that she needs some time before she is ready to proceed with the police investigation.    At this point the case will be placed as Ex-Cleared/Closed pending the victim wanting to proceed with the police investigation with the assistance of BCPO.    (See BCPO Case #BCP-25-00237)

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Tuesday, March 4, 2025, at approximately 15:28 hours, P.O. Scangarella and I responded to 195 High Street on a report of a dispute involving a baseball bat. Upon arrival, I met with the victims, Albert Hunt and Melody Foster in front of the listed address. Foster reported that earlier today, at approximately 06:30 hours, she heard loud banging on the front door. She reported that she did not answer the door but believed it was the suspect because she owed him money.  She indicated that the knocking eventually stopped. I then spoke with Hunt who reported that, while walking home from work, in the area of First and High Street, a white BMW sedan pulled along side of him. He further reported that the suspect exited the BMW and confronted him. Hunt reported that the suspect began asking where Foster was and Hunt advised him that he believes she was at the listed address. The suspect then stated " Ya keep fucking around with me and I am going to bust a cap in both of your asses'. Hunt further reported that, as he continued walking towards his house, the suspect reentered the white BMW and began following him. Hunt indicated that, upon arriving to his residence, the suspect exited the BMW, and followed him up the front porch. Hunt added that, upon opening his front door, the suspect followed him inside, uninvited. Hunt reported that, upon entering behind him, the suspect removed a short black baseball bat from his waistband and stated " I will fuck both of ya up" and began demanding the money Foster owed him.     Hunt reported that, he told the suspect that he was going to call the police. The suspect then stated ' I ain't scared of no police" and attempted to snatch Hunts phone from his hands. Hunt indicated that he was able to recover the cell phone and the suspect fled. Following the incident, Hunt and Foster observed one of their windows shattered. Both victims alleged that the suspect was responsible for the broken window, however, neither of them observed it. Foster provided a NJ registration for the suspect's vehicle of NJ -D78PNR. A computer search of the registration revealed it belonged to a White BMW 430. Foster also provided the suspects name and an address of 26 Anderson Street, where she believes the mother resides. The results of the registration lookup is also associated with the aforementioned address.     Hunt was willing to provide a recorded statement of the incident and I transported him to headquarters. Upon arrival, Det. Mora responded and escorted Hunt to the interview to complete the statement. Hunt also stated he wanted to sign complaints against the suspect. Victim Notification form was completed and a copy was provided to the vicim. Lt. Bay authored complaint number 2025-0223-W-000101 on my behalf against the suspect for 2C:15-1A(2), 2C:12-3A, 2C:12-1A3, 2C:39-5D, and 2C:39-4D.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect description:  BM, 5'5, gray vest, blue hooded sweatshirt, blue jeans, black medium length hair, black purse and a black backpack.       I responded to the listed location on a report of a shoplifting.  Upon arrival, I spoke with Employee Digeshkum Patel who advised a black male (suspect) had entered his store whom he recognized that had caused a disturbance earlier in the day with his customers.  He told the suspect to leave or he will call the police.  The suspect then grabbed two 50 ml bottles of Patron Silver and the employee grabbed his hand.  A short tug of war ensued resulting in the employee suffering an abrasion to his left thumb.  The suspect then left the store with the bottles, picked up a black backpack he left outside the store and walked North on Lehigh Street.  I asked the employee if the suspect said anything during the theft and he stated the suspect responded, "If you call the police, I will kill you."  For clarification, I later asked him at what point the threats occurred and the second time he replied that the threats occurred just prior to the suspect taking the bottles.       The victim declined medical attention and I requested a Supervisor to respond.  Sgt. Briggs responded and asked the victim if he was able to make a recorded statement which he replied that he could come to the police department tomorrow.  I asked the victim if he believed if the threat to his life would be carried out or if he was in fear for his safety and he replied "No."       A BOLO of the suspect was put out to patrols and a canvass of the area was negative for the suspect.       The victim did want to file charges against the suspect if he was identified.  A victim notification form was completed and provided to the victim.  The employee was asked to retain the video footage of the incident until a detective could make a copy.          The bottles of Patron were valued at $7.00 each for a total loss of $14.00.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On March 21, 2025 at approximately 14:19 hours, I responded to 171 Kenneth St (Rocky Auto Repair Shop) for a motor vehicle theft report. Upon arrival, I met with the shop owner, Wilfredo Polanco, who called on behalf of the vehicle's owner.      Mr. Polanco reported that the vehicle was dropped off on 03/20/2025 for service by John Velez, a church volunteer. Mr. Polanco further reported that on 03/20/205 at approximately 17:00 hours, he parked the church van outside in the shop driveway and placed the keys in the visor. Mr. Polanco reported that this is common practice between him and the church since a member of the church normally picks up the van after hours.      A church administrator, Ms. Wass reached out to Mr. Martin and advised him that she observed the vehicle in East Rutherford, NJ and wanted to know why it was there. This alerted Mr. Martin to the fact that the vehicle was possibly moved without his or the church's authorization.      Mr. Martin then reached out to Mr. Polanco on 03/21/2025 at approximately 13:00 hours, and questioned why the church's van was in East Rutherford. Mr. Polanco reported he did not know as he believed the van was picked up as usual. Mr. Polanco proceeded to check security cameras for the auto shop where he learned the van was stolen on 03/21/2025 at approximately 07:50 hours.      I watched the security footage where I observed a white female, wearing blue sweatpants, a black sweater with the hood up, with blonde hair walk up to the van. After a few moments, the suspect enters the van. The suspect proceeds to sit in the van for a couple of minutes before starting the vehicle South on Kenneth St. Mr. Polanco was advised to save all footage.      Mr. Martin requested the vehicle to be processed if found. A consent to search form was filled out and signed.      The vehicle was entered into the ATTF system.     Communication was provided the vehicle information to be entered into NCIC.     Sgt. Tripodi was notified by Sgt. Compitiello.     Value of the vehicle is estimated to be $4,000.00.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect Vehicle Description: White 4-door SUV, possibly a Honda CRV or Toyota style SUV.      I responded to the listed location on a reported burglary from auto. Upon arrival, I spoke with the victim. He stated that, on 03/23/2025 at approximately 0415 hours, he had awoke to the sound of his vehicle alarm sounding. He further stated that when he looked outside, he observed the suspect vehicle parked next to his, facing west. The victim reported that upon seeing him, the suspect vehicle fled the scene, traveling west on Pulaski Place towards an unknown direction. He further stated that he had entered the vehicle via the driver side prior to my arrival and located a wrench in the driver seat that was not his. He reported that the suspect also damaged the steering wheel and ignition system to his vehicle.        Upon confirming that the victim did not want the vehicle processed, he entered his vehicle and verified that no items of his were missing. The victim reported that a cane, located in the console between the driver and passenger seats, was not his. The cane and wrench were then secured as evidence.        P.O. Rodriguez responded with the patrol camera on scene. I then took seven photos (placard included) of the scene. It should be noted that I secured the cane prior to P.O. Rodriguez's arrival on scene. The original position of the cane was captured on my body worn camera.        A search of the area for cameras yielded the following: 32 Pulaski Place, 29 Pulaski Place,18 Pulaski Place,17 Pulaski Place, and 15 Pulaski Place.       The victim stated that the approximate cost to repair his vehicle is $1,000.00.         A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and provided to the victim. He was advised on how to obtain a copy of  this report.      The cane and wrench were secured in evidence locker number one.Insurance Info: 908340839Company Code: 135

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect: Hispanic female approximately 50 years of age and heavy build.      On March 25, 2025, at approximately 1204 hours I responded to 450 Hackensack Avenue on a reported robbery. Sgt. Donnerstag and Sgt. Ghirardi responded to assist. We spoke with the victim who reported that he had just finished shopping at 99 Ranch Market and placed the groceries in his vehicle. After placing the groceries in his vehicle, the victim sat in the driver's seat of his car. The victim further reported he was approached by the suspect who put a gold chain around his neck and began to converse with him. The suspect then began to rip the victim's Rolex watch off of his left wrist. The victim attempted to stop the suspect but was unsuccessful. The suspect stole the watch and got into the passenger seat of a white sedan, which drove off. The victim sustained abrasions to both hands.     The Rolex stolen was gold and had a white face. The approximate value is $15,000.00. Hackensack University Medical Center BLS responded to the scene and evaluated the patient. The patient refused to be transported to the hospital and signed a form. Sgt. Williams and Det. Tanucilli responded to the scene to assist. The victim reported that he would provide a recorded statement to detectives at a later date because he had to go home to tend to his son. The victim did not want his vehicle processed at this point in time. There are surveillance cameras where this incident occurred. I completed a victim notification form and the victim was given a copy.    The necklace that was placed on the victim's neck was secured in an evidence bag and transported to Hackensack Police Headquarters where it was placed in evidence locker #8. TOT to detectives.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On March 28th, 2025 P.O. McCarthy, P.O. Salas, P.O. Francavilla, S.P.O. Aster, and SGT. Tovbin responded to the listed location on a report of a suspicious incident. Upon arrival, we met with the complainant who had live footage of the listed location through her ring camera of the arrestee inside. P.O. Salas P.O. Francavilla and S.P.O. Aster created a perimeter on the outside of the building and P.O. McCarthy, SGT. Tovbin and I entered the location. Once we reached suite 202 and realized we could not gain entry, P.O. Francavilla responded with a key provided by the complainant.     SGT. Tovbin and P.O. McCarthy entered Suite 202 identifying themselves and giving loud verbal commands for the arrestee to show himself. Shortly after entering the suite to conduct a search, the arrestee attempted to exit using the second door. I advised him to show his hands, and P.O. Francavilla and myself were able to handcuff him for our safety after a brief struggle.    We escorted the arrestee outside, where we searched his person for our safety.  We then asked him for his pedigree information to which he gave us the name "Jorge Williams".  We were unable to locate any person with the information provided. Believing that he was providing us with false pedigree information and hindering our investigation, we determined it was necessary to transport him to headquarters for fingerprinting to confirm his identity.  While S.P.O. Abueg and I were escorting him to radio car 111, the arrestee began running, initiating a foot pursuit. He fled South in the parking lot and then ran across the street into the lot of 80 South Newman Street. P.O. McCarthy deployed his taser, but it did not make contact with his person due to the several layers of clothing he was wearing. P.O. Francavilla was able to apprehend the arrestee and bring him to the ground.     The arrestee was then placed in radio car 108 and transported to police headquarters by P.O. McCarthy. The vehicle was checked before and after transportation which yielded negative results.      Prior to transportation, the complainant approached us and advised us that the arrestee had stolen and eaten a cake they had in their break room. The complainant stated that the stolen cake was valued at $26.99. Melanie Esquiveo then responded to police headquarters to provide a statement to detectives.     Upon arrival at Police Headquarters, he was searched again by P.O. Vicale and handcuffed to the bench. The arrestee then began cooperating and provided us with the correct pedigree information which yielded an active warrant out of Passaic.      The arrestee complained of difficulty breathing and an ambulance was requested. Hackensack Ambulance arrived on scene and assessed the arrestee's. They stated his heart rate was a bit accelerated but should calm down momentarily. After the arrestee took off his two sweatshirts and 2 shirts, he started calming down. The arrestee refused further medical attention.    P.O. Vicale and P.O. Salas processed the arrestee with no complications.    The arrestee was found in possession of an American Express Amex Card which was secured as evidence and placed into evidence locker 2.    The arrestee was charged with:       Burglary - 2C:18-2       Hindering Apprehension - 2C:29-3       Resisting Arrest/Eluding - 2C:29-2A(2)       Obstruction - 2C:29-1(B)    P.O. Anguizaca and P.O. Vicale transported the arrestee to BCJ without further incident.     See Detectives supplement for further.
ALL_INCIDENTS: Burglary - Commercial - 2C:18-2 - Hindering Apprehension - 2C:29-3 - Resisting Arrest/Eluding - 2C:29-2

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On March 28th, 2025, at approximately 1110 hours, P.O. Lyak, Sgt. Donnerstag, Det./Sgt. Williams and I, responded to 2 Park Avenue, Apt. 635 (Ivy & Green), on a report of a burglary. Upon our arrival we met with the victim, Juan Rondon Jr., who stated that his apartment was burglarized.      The victim stated he left his apartment on March 27th, 2025, at approximately 23:30 hours and returned to his apartment the following morning on March 28th, at approximately 0830 hours. When the victim arrived at his apartment, he noticed the door was slightly opened. Upon entry, he noticed the dead bolt to the apartment door was forced open and broken. The victim stated he had locked the dead bolt and door handle lock prior to leaving the night of March 27th, but did not utilize the door latch.      The suspect stated after observing the broken door, he entered his apartment and found drawers, cabinets, and his bedroom door were opened. The victim noticed black heavy-duty garbage bags located in the center of his apartment that were left by the suspects. While attempting to identify if merchandise or valuables were stolen during the burglary, he opened and closed many of the drawers and cabinets, contaminating the scene.     The victim stated, approximately $10,000.00 dollars in the denomination of $20.00 dollar bills located in the a kitchen drawer was missing.      Upon further examination, the victim stated a Two-Tone Sky Dweller Champaign Dial Rolex, model #326933, serial #54XV1419, valued at approximately $20,000.00 dollars was stolen. The watch was located inside the pocket of a pair of jeans in his bedroom closet. It should be noted, the victim's closet where the watch was stolen was in the same condition as when the suspect departed his residence. No other belongings or merchandise were disturbed or moved. The suspects appeared to only have gone into a select pair of jeans which contained the Rolex watch.      A Jubilee fourteen (14) karat gold bracelet valued at approximately $8,000.00 dollars was stolen. The bracelet was located on the top of the TV stand.      A fourteen (14) karat white gold necklace valued at approximately $8,000.00 dollars was stolen. The necklace was located in the apartment bedroom.      Multiple FedEx packages containing high-end clothing items and clothing items were stolen from the apartment bedroom and closet. The packages were valued at approximately $4,000.00 dollars.   A Nintendo Switch (Gaming Console) valued at approximately $300.00 dollars was stolen. The gaming console was located on the living room couch.      Detective Sergeant Williams met with the building concierge and they were able show security footage of three (3) unknown individuals wearing black clothing, hoods, face masks, and black gloves. The suspects entered stairwell 3, located across from the victim's apartment at approximately 0030 hours. The suspects were then viewed leaving the apartment approximately six (6) minutes later at 0036 hours. For further information with regards to the suspects involved, please refer to Detective Sergeant Williams case report.      The victim was provided with a Victim Notification Form and wished to sign complaints against the suspects if identified.

Narrative: On March 31st, 2025, I responded to the listed address (100 Essex Street) on a report of a burglary. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On April 2, 2025, at approximately 2140 hours P.O. Bonsu and I responded to 260 Union Avenue on a reported mental health incident. We spoke with Janice Reid, who is a supervisor at the Holly Center, and she reported that A.M.C. was arguing with O.L.. They began to fight and the Holly Center staff were able to separate both of them. After the fight, A.M.C began becoming verbally aggressive with the staff and wrote a note that stated, "You dumb bitch's I ain't telling yall that yall but I going my gang to shoot this place up." Janice Reid further reported that that A.M.C. kept threatening to get a gang to shoot up the Holly Center.       While on scene, A.M.C. made statements that she wanted "to go down for attempted murder" and that she wanted to get CPC High Point shot up. CPC High Point is a school A.M.C. previously attended. Then A.M.C. began to state that she was sexually assaulted by Holly Center staff and specifically named Tyrone Bagley and Randall White who were on scene. Sgt. Donnerstag and Sgt. Ghirardi responded to the scene to assist. P.O. Bonsu asked A.M.C. more about her allegations. A.M.C. reported the incident occurred approximately a month ago on a weekend but did not recall if it was in the morning, afternoon, or evening. A.M.C. further reported it happened in her room at the Holly Center but she did not want to discuss any further. Hackensack University Medical Center BLS responded and transported A.M.C. to Hackensack University Medical Center. Janice Reid accompanied A.M.C.. I followed the ambulance to Hackensack University Medical Center.      Detective Garrett was notified and responded to Hackensack University Medical Center. I notified Alexandra #1576 from DCP&P of the incident. She reported that DCP&P would be making an immediate response to the hospital. Detective Garrett stated he was familiar with the patient and of the prior sexual assault allegation from previous encounters.  T.O.T. to detectives. See supplement for further contact information. A CBH care form was completed.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Friday, April 4, 2025, at approximately 13:15 hours, I responded in marked radio car #108 to 75 Fair Street, Apt. 1, for a report of a burglary to a residence.  Upon arrival, I spoke to SANDY JOHNSON, the victim's neighbor, who stated that the listed residence was burglarized.     JOHNSON stated that the victim has been at New Bridge Medical Center's intensive care unit for over month and she has permission from the victim to check on her apartment.  JOHNSON further stated that she regularly checks the victim's mailbox and puts the victim's mail inside the victim's apartment which is reported to be left unlocked at all times.  On Tuesday, 04/01/2025 at approximately 12:00 hours, JOHNSON entered the apartment to drop off the victim's mail and check the apartment, specifically checking to make sure that the TV in the victim's bedroom was still there.  The TV was last seen at this time.  On today's date at approximately 12:30 hours,  JOHNSON entered the apartment and noticed that the TV in the victim's bedroom was missing.  JOHNSON left the apartment and notified the complainant (victim's son).       I requested a supervisor to which Sgt. Tovbin responded.  Upon his arrival, we cleared the apartment which yielded negative results for unwanted persons.  Sgt. Tovbin notified Det. Sgt. Tripodi regarding the incident.  Det. Sgt. Tripodi received consent to process the scene from the complainant on the victim's behalf via telephone.  Det. Sgt. Tripodi, Det. G. Lara-Nunez and Det. Dogali responded on scene.  Bergen County Sheriff's Office BCI Unit responded on scene.  Det. Colon #1952 photographed the scene, see BCI case number #2025-0211.  The door to the apartment was secured by JOHNSON.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect description: Slim build, black male, approximately 5'11 in height, wearing all black / dark clothing.     On 4/8/25 at approximately 00:52 hours,  I responded to the area of 120 Atlantic Street on a reported suspicious person. It was reported an unknown male suspect attempted to open a vehicle door and fled the area on foot. P.O. Papanikolaou and Sgt. Butler responded to assist.   Upon arrival I met with the complainant / witness, Sherif Abraham. The complainant reported on 4/8/25 at approximately 00:47 hours, he observed the suspect inside vehicle 1 (NJ T21VBK). Vehicle 1 was parked curbside on the north side of Atlantic Street, in front of 118 Atlantic Street. The complainant further reported the suspect fled the area on foot traveling north, last seen jumping the fence of 118 Atlantic Street. The complainant reported while the suspect fled, he dropped a black duffle bag, which was located on the west side of 118 Atlantic Street.  Upon inspecting vehicle 1, the interior did not appear disturbed. Upon further investigation, the front passenger side door to vehicle 1 was left insecure. The front passenger door to vehicle 1 was unable to be secured. Vehicle 1 also had a dash camera fixed to the front windshield. At this time it was unknown if the dash camera was recording / operable.  Upon responding to the west side of 118 Atlantic Street, I observed a medium size black in color "Jordan" duffle bag containing clothing / property. Upon further checking the area, a tied / knotted mint colored small size towel was also observed. All found property was secured in radio car 104.   A DMV check of vehicle 1 was conducted, which resulted the registered owner, Luis Moreno Colmenares, resided at 107 Atlantic Street. P.O. Papanikolaou later responded to 107 Atlantic Street in attempt to locate / speak with the owner, which resulted negative.  A Hackensack Notification Card was completed and secured on vehicle 1. Additionally a Hackensack Notification Card was completed and secured in the mail box of 107 Atlantic Street.   P.O. Estrada and P.O. Mena checked the area in an attempt to locate the suspect, which resulted negative.   At this time, I was unable to confirm vehicle 1 was burglarized or what proceeds may have been stolen from the vehicle. All evidence / found property was transported to headquarters. At headquarters all property was inventoried.   The medium size black in color "Jordan" duffle bag containing a white in color Adidas hat, a pair of white and blue colored socks, black colored shorts, white colored shirt, clear glass bottle of cologne / perfume and a pair of white and orange colored puma sneakers.  The tied / knotted mint colored small size towel contained a black plastic organizing tray inside. Inside the tray contained the following: - A black  plastic ash tray cup- Juicy J's rolling paper(s)- "OCB" slim premium rolling paper(s) with tip(s)- Three multi color plastic lighters - White in color USB car charger - A backwoods magnet - Two "UFOND" headband lights - White colored paper matches - A silver and black in color "driven" ammunition style key chain- $4.53 in assorted change- A small clear glass bottle with a silver in color metal screw cap. Attached to the screw cap was a small silver in color spoon / scooper. Within the bottle contained an unknown white powdered substance (suspected cocaine).    The mint in color towel containing multiple items was later placed in a brown paper bag. The currency was later placed in currency security bag # 0588856. The small clear glass bottle containing the suspected cocaine was later placed in evidence bag # 7824140. All evidence / found property was later secured in evidence locker 6. See evidence report for further.   The immediate area was checked for any other vehicles possibly burglarized with negative results.
ALL_INCIDENTS: Burglary - Auto - 2C:18-2 - Suspicious Person - Property - Found

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On April 13, 2025, at approximately 1634 hours, P.O. McCarthy and I were dispatched to 157 Central Ave for a parking complaint. Upon our arrival, arrestee 1, Melanie Curry,  arrestee 2, Tyrone Miles, victim 1, Edwin Vasquez, and victim 2, Mirelis Lopez, were standing in the rear parking lot of 157 Central Ave arguing.  I spoke with arrestee 1, Melanie Curry, who stated that the victim, Edwin Lopez, has been parking in her parking spot, which has been an ongoing problem. Arrestee 1 also stated that she went into the rear seat of the Ford Explorer and took victim 2's pocketbook. She further stated the reason she took the pocketbook was so they would move their vehicle. I spoke with Victim 1, who stated that he was arguing with arrestee 1 and arrestee 2 over parking. Arrestee 2 walked over to the victim's 2005 Honda Accord, silver in color, New Jersey Registration N51UXW, and punctured both front tires with a knife.  I spoke with victim 2, who stated that arrestee 2 walked over to her 2019 Ford Explorer, black in color, New Jersey Registration D478UTT, which was also parked in the parking lot. The arrestee opened the rear door without permission and took out her Michael Kors black pocketbook from the back seat, and brought it into her apartment. See below for a detailed list of the contents of the purse. Victim 2 showed us a video of arrestee 2 puncturing the front tires of victim 1's vehicle. The video shows a black male wearing different clothing than what arrestee 2 was wearing, but you could see that arrestee 2 had the same wedding ring and blue bracelet that he had on his left wrist. Victim 2 further stated that arrestee 2 went into his residence and changed his clothes and sneakers.  Sgt. Briggs and P.O. Marble arrived on the scene to assist. Sgt. Brigg advised me that while he was speaking with arrestee 2, the arrestee admitted that he did puncture the two front tires with a knife. Sgt. Briggs went with the arrestee and retrieved the knife, described at a 10" kitchen knife with a blue handle and five inch blade which he turned over to me to be secured and transported to headquarters.   Both arrestees were placed in handcuffs, arrestee 1 was placed in the rear of patrol vehicle 108, and arrestee 2 was placed in the rear of patrol vehicle 107, Both vehicles were checked before and after for contraband. Both arrestees were searched at the scene and back at headquarters with negative results for additional contraband.  Victim notification forms were filled out and provided to both victims. A Handle with Care form was completed and turned into the front desk.  The knife was placed in an evidence knife box, tagged and placed into evidence locker 1  I contacted DCPP and spoke with Alisha #1357 and informed her of the situation. She advised me that they would not be responding but would be documenting the incident at this time.  Vehicle 1: cost of two tires $300.00 eachGeico Insurance 6168591045 The following items were taken from Vehicle 2 Black Michael Kors pocketbook $300.00wallet                                          $50.00Cash                                           $70.00 Diapers                                       $50.00Medicationa single blank check for the victim's checking account which was loose in the purse. Detective Tanucilli was notified and responded to headquarters to take recorded statements. See his report for further.  Both suspects were processed without incident and Lt. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On April 13, 2025 at approximately 18:44 hours, P.O. Alvarez, SPO Abueg, and I responded to 64 prospect ave apt #B9 for a 911 call for a possible fight.     Upon arrival, I observed a black male, wearing a black leather jacket with white on the boarders, grey jeans, black and red shoes, with short hair, running out of the apartment complex. I attempted to stop the party but he fled running to the rear of 77 Prospect Ave. As I was unaware if the person who fled was involved in the incident I responded to apartment #B9 where I observed the door half open, glass on the floor, and the apartment to be in disarray.     At that moment, Anthony Campbell (victim hereafter), walked out. I observed a laceration to his forehead. The victim also complained of right leg pain. I observed a laceration to his right leg. Hackensack EMS #134 were on scene. They evaluated the victim and he signed an RMA form.     The victim reported he was sitting in his apartment when he began to hear loud banging. The victim reported he made his way to the front door to investigate the sound. The victim reported as he approached the door, it swung open violently. The victim reported he then saw Michael Dearmas (arrestee hereafter) standing on the outside. It should be noted that Dearmas matched the description of the person who fled upon my arrival to the building. The victim reported the arrestee then ran into the apartment and grabbed him by the shirt striking him in the head in the process. The victim reported he then attempted to back up to call 911 to which the arrestee then yelled " I am going to kill you ". The victim reported the arrestee then ran out of the apartment.      The victim reported damage to the front door which would cost approximately $50.00 to repair. The victim also reported a broken mirror which cost him approximately $150.00.     The victim reported he has never had issues with the arrestee until the divorce proceeding with his daughter was initiated. Sgt. Ghirardi arrived on scene. Sgt. Ghirardi notified Det. Tanucilli. Det. Tanucilli arrived on scene.  I asked the victim if he wished to have the scene processed to which he accepted. A Hackensack Police Consent to Search form was completed and turned over to Det. Tanucilli upon his arrival on scene. BCSO Det. S. Delacruz #1955 arrived on scene. Det. Delacruz photographed and processed the scene. See BCSO case number #2025-0233 for more.       While on scene, Sgt. Ghirardi was speaking to the victims daughter on the phone when she received a call from the arrestee. The victims daughter placed the call on 3-way where he disclosed he was standing in front of a liquor store on Lehigh St. Communications was able to confirm the arrestees location via security cameras. P.O. Vicale, SPO Abueg, P.O. Voorhis, P.O. Caero, and P.O. Alvarez made their way to the arrestees location. Upon arrival, the arrestee again fled on an electric bicycle heading south into the NJ Transit Station Lot on Essex St. P.O. Vicale activated his exterior emergency warning lights in attempted to stop the arrestee.     Upon reaching the back of the parking lot the arrestee jumped off the bicycle and ran onto the train tracks. The arrestee then jumped into a small creek but surrendered to P.O. Vicale shortly after. The arrestee was pulled out of the creek by P.O. Vicale and handcuffed behind his back. The arrestee then requested an ambulance for leg pain. The arrestee was transported to Hackensack Hospital. The arrestee was searched before and after the transported which yielded negative results for contraband. P.O. Voorhis rode in the ambulance with the arrestee while P.O. Vicale followed.     At approximately 21:00 the victim contacted the Hackensack Police Headquarters advising he had located the arrestees phone inside the apartment. I responded to the apartment to secure the phone as evidence. The phone was tagged and secured in evidence locker #2.      Communication confirmed the arrestee had active warrant #W-2025-000167 out of this jurisdiction.        The arrestee also had warrant # W-2025-000078 and 1604-W-2025-000080 from Hawthrone PD.     The victims daughter, Toya Campbell-Dearmas, had active FRO #FV-14-001021-25 against the arrestee. The victims address is listed on the FRO.      The bicycle the arrestee was on was logged into property and secured in bulk storage in the old police garage.       A New Jersey Victim Notification form was filled out and a copy was provided to the victim.      Lt. Klosk authored complainant #W 2025 000178 0223 on my behalf for the charges of 2C:18-2, 2C:12-3, 2C12-1a, 2C:17-3, 2C:25-31.
ALL_INCIDENTS: Burglary - Residence - 2C:18-2 - Terroristic Threats - 2C:12-3 - Criminal Mischief - 2C:17-3

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect: White Hispanic male, approximately 30-40 years old, facial hair, named "Jean"     On 04/14/25, I responded to police headquarters for a walk-in that reported being sexually assaulted on 03/23/2025. The victim reported that on 03/23/25 she requested a 'Lyft' ride from her residence to her friend's house that resides at 169 Passaic Street, Hackensack. The Lyft ride arrived at the victim's residence at approximately 14:53 hours.     During the course of the transportation, the victim believed she had left her cellphone at home. The suspect offered to return to retrieve her cellphone. The victim then realizes her cellphone was in her bag that was inside the vehicle. The victim apologized multiple times to the suspect for returning to her home. The victim then offered $40.00 in cash to the suspect who then accepted the cash.      During the course of the transportation, the victim reported the suspect was asking her personal questions such as: "when was the last time you kissed someone and/or had sex?" The victim stated "about a year and a half ago or more". The suspect pulled over near Jefferson Street. The suspect then stated "kiss me ,come to the front seat and kiss me". The victim then entered the front seat. The suspect then grabbed the victim's face and kissed her for approximately 10 seconds. While kissing the victim, the suspect groped the victim's chest from outer clothing, then down to her hips, to her vagina. The suspect then rubbed the victim's vagina over her clothing. The victim stated she was "frozen" and unable to react to the suspects action. The suspect then stopped and asked to kiss her again. The victim stated the second kiss lasted 2-3 seconds. The victim stated to the suspect she had bad experiences in the past and did not feel comfortable continuing. The suspect then stopped; however, kept his right hand on her thighs.     Over the course of the ride, the suspect attempted to pull over again but this time on Prospect Avenue and Atlantic Street. The victim's friend called her during this time,  questioning her about her location due to the time that she had left from home. Upon arrival to her destination, as the victim exited the vehicle, the suspect stated "You can call me if you want to get a better experience."     A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed. A copy was given and explained to the victim. The Detective Bureau was notified and Det. Carroll responded to speak with the victim.  Reference initial reported taken by River Edge Police on 4/12/2025: Case # 2025-03824

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: P.O. Mattalian and I responded to the listed location on a report of a burglary that took place in the past.  The victim's residence is occupied as a rooming house with multiple tenants. Upon arrival, we made contact with the victim, Gerard White.  The victim stated that he left his residence yesterday 4/14/2025 at approximately 2300 hours. The victim had slept in a hotel due to him and his girlfriend (Erica Best) getting into a verbal altercation. The victim stated Best was not in the room when he left to the hotel.  The victim returned back to his home today 4/15/2025 at approximately 1830 hours and was unable to open his door due to unknown object blocking his room door. The victim walked to the back of the structure to look through his window. The victim discovered his room window was ajar which appeared suspicious. The victim further discovered his apartment in complete disarray, with furniture flipped upside down, and tomato sauce smeared all over his walls.  The victim then discovered his pillow and some personal belongings in the garbage can that was across the driveway in the rear of the home.  Sgt. Competiello arrived on scene.     An anonymous neighbor from room # 5 advised us that at approximately 0400 hours on 4/15/2025 he observed the silhouette of a women dressed like a "ninja" coming from the rear of the listed location with unknown items. The female then entered into an unknown SUV.  The victim stated that he was missing a pair of suede "Jordan" shoes with an approximate cost of $300.00.  He also had damage to his dresser and bed that had an approximate cost of $550.00.  P.O. Mattalian was able to pry the bedroom door open and I observed tomato sauce and food smeared all over the walls of the room.  Det./Sgt. Williams and Det. Tanucilli arrived on scene.  The victim gave us verbal and written consent to have the room processed.  Det. Brielle Colon # 1952 from BCI arrived on scene to photograph and fingerprint the scene.  The victim was given a copy of the victim notification form and advised that detectives would follow up with him.        ****See Detective Tanucilli's supplement***

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: I responded to 130 Overlook Avenue for a report of a burglary.  Upon arrival, I met with the complainant in the lobby of the apartment building. She stated that she believes the suspect had stolen her wallet from her apartment.      The victim stated that approximately a week and a half ago the incident had occurred.  The victim was unable to provide a specific date and time of the incident.  The suspect is a neighbor of the victim and has had interactions with him in the past.  She further stated that on the day of the incident, the suspect appeared in her residence uninvited and did not see him come through the front door.  When inside, the suspect began asking the victim questions about her carpet and couch.  The victim said after he asked the questions, the suspect left.  After the incident, she realized her wallet was missing from her dining room table.  The victim reported that the wallet is of the brand Victoria's Secret which is black in color with a flower design (worth approximately $30.00), containing two NJ Transit bus tickets and approximately $7.00 in coin currency. She stated that she did not see the suspect take the wallet.  No signs of forced entry were reported to the victim's apartment door.      The suspect was on scene and he stated that he has never gone into the victim's residence, invited or uninvited.  The only time he's interacted with her was a few months ago he gave the victim containers of food after the victim had surgery.  The suspect stated that the victim might be going through early stages of dementia.A New Jersey Victim Notification form was completed and a copy was given to the victim.I notified Sgt. Tovbin who advised Det. Sgt. Williams of the incident.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Monday, April 21, 2025 at approximately 0800 hours,  P.O Francavilla and I responded to 16 Johnson Avenue on a report of a burglary.     Upon my arrival, I spoke to the complainant who escorted P.O. Francavilla and myself to the rear of the building where a window appeared to be forced open. I then spoke to Giustina who stated she arrived at the business and unlocked the door to set up the office. She advised that once she entered, she realized the alarm was going off and then noticed 3 alcohol bottles on the floor. Giustina stated that once she noticed the bottles, she exited the business and waited in the parking lot. P.O Francavilla and I made entry into the business and completed an interior check.  The interior check yielded negative results for any person(s) in the business.  While completing the interior check, I observed a safe on the floor pried open and the main office was torn apart. Upon further inspection, I observed that several other rooms had cabinets opened. Giustina then handed us the key for the outside gate that lead to the opened window. Upon further investigation, we noticed a crow bar on the floor next to the window. I requested for a supervisor and SGT. Tovbin arrived on scene. SGT. Tovbin requested detectives and DET. Dogali and D/SGT. Williams arrived on scene at approximately 0855 hours.    The complainant consented to the business to be processed and signed a Consent to Search form.    At 0850 hours, the scene was preserved and a crime scene log had commenced.-0908 hours, Det. Charlenea/Det. Delacruz/ D/SGT. Dilkes (BCI) arrived to process the scene.-1014 hours, detectives were completed and the scene was turned over to D/SGT. Williams and DET. Dogali.  Giustina advised $4,000.00 were taken but that she was unaware what else was taken.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On April 29, 2025, I responded to the listed location for a report of a stolen vehicle.  Upon arrival, I spoke to the victim who stated her vehicle was stolen from the guest parking lot.     The victim stated that she parked her vehicle in guest spot #2 facing north at approximately 14:00 hours. The victim returned to her vehicle at approximately 14:40 hours where she realized her vehicle was missing.  Upon review of the surveillance footage, the vehicle was stolen at 14:15 hours.  At 14:14 hours, a blue colored SUV with front flashing lights (unknown registration, make or model) was seen pulling up to the listed vehicle and two white male parties exited.  The driver of the blue colored SUV entered the listed vehicle then fled in the vehicle and was last seen fleeing south on Prospect Avenue.  The other male party got into the driver seat of the blue SUV and also fled south on Prospect Avenue.       The victim was unable to articulate if she had left her vehicle unlocked and/or running but stated that she did not leave the key fob in the vehicle.   The victim stated that she had property that was left in the vehicle which included: a MCM handbag color brown (worth $500.00), her NJ driver's license, a Capital One credit card, a Wells Fargo debit card, hers and her daughter's birth certificates, Social Security card, U.S. Passport, and a car seat (worth approximately $50.00).  The estimated cost for the listed vehicle is $21,000.00. A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and a copy was given to the victim.  The victim stated she does not want her vehicle processed.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: I responded to the listed location on theft of sneakers.  Upon arrival I made contact with the victim, Matese Shine, who stated that 15 pairs of her Jordan and Nike sneakers were missing from her home.  The victim stated that she has been staying at her boyfriends house but came back to her home this morning at approximately 1100 hours and realized a few of her sneaker boxes were missing. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On 5/9/25 while at 500 South River Street Sgt. Competiello and I were approached by an unknown party stating a shoplifter was getting physical with ShopRite Loss Prevention Officer(s). Sgt. Competiello responded and made contact with the all parties. The suspect / arrestee, Virgil Kearney, was then placed under arrest. It was reported on 5/9/25 the victim / ShopRite Loss Prevention Officer observed Kearney on video surveillance conceal multiple items / merchandise in his pocket / backpack. Kearney was later observed walking towards the front register area and took two Red Bull drinks. Kearney is seen passing all points of sale(s), failing to pay, and exiting the store. While outside the store, ShopRite Loss Prevention approached Kearney. A physical altercation then started when Kearney tackled the victim, Conner Maret, to the ground and began wrestling the victim, while on the ground. Due to the physical altercation the victim sustained a minor scratch to his neck, left side. A witness / ShopRite Loss Prevention Officer, Martin Almonte-Tejada, was also present.Prior to transport to headquarters, Kearney was searched and multiple stolen merchandise were located in his pockets. All known stolen merchandise were later returned to ShopRite Loss Prevention. At this time the total amount of stolen proceed(s) were unknown, but ShopRite Loss Prevention estimated to be approximately $60.00 to $200.00.The arrestee was searched incident to arrest, yielding negative results for contraband. The arrestee was transported to Headquarters in marked radio car #115. P.O. Mattalian followed behind. Concurrently, Sgt. Competiello transported the victim to Headquarters in marked radio car #109. Upon arrival at Headquarters, the victim was turned over to Detective Dogali to provide a recorded statement. See Det. Dogali's supplement for further. The arrestee was fingerprinted and photographed.  I authored Complaint W 2025 000233 0223 for 2C:15-1A(1) robbery. The arrestee was then remanded to the Bergen County Jail.Due to the arrestee currently being homeless CBH Care / ARRIVE was offered, which he accepted. A CBH Care Form was later completed.Be advised, while issuing a city ordinance violation at the same physical location (Case # 25-038212), Sgt. Competiello and I were approached by a party who advised us of the Robbery in progress at Shop Rite. While Sgt. Competiello responded to Shop Rite, I quickly completed my city ordinance violation call, deactivated my BWC and responded to assist. Upon responding to Shop Rite I thought I activated my BWC, but it was later discovered It was never activated.
