Narrative: On Monday, May 12th, 2025, I responded to the listed location on a burglary report to auto report. Upon arrival, I met with Isaac Martinezestrada (complainant hereafter) of vehicle 1 bearing New Jersey Plate P36RMF, which was parked in parking space 95. The complainant stated he parked the vehicle at approximately 19:00 on 05/11/2025 and was unsure if he had locked the doors. He further stated that he returned to it at approximately 06:45 on 05/12/2025. He also reported that when he returned to the vehicle, he noticed the driver's side door slightly ajar, with his charging cable outside the car. The complainant reported that he noticed his car was rummaged through, and $18.00 was missing from the driver's side compact. He further noted that nothing else was taken, and his vehicle was intact. The complainant also reported having a dash camera facing outwards of the vehicle. He further reported that at approximately 03:21 hours on 05/12/2025, he could hear a person/suspect rummaging through the vehicle. He was advised to save the audio. Sgt. Ghirardi was notified.  I completed a victim's notification form and provided a copy to the complainant. The complainant wanted to sign a complaint if the suspect was to be identified.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: The suspect is the victim's mother's boyfriend's son, who is not related to the victim and does not reside at the residence. The victim is not friends with the suspect but only knows him.          On Monday, May 12th, 2025, P.O. Lyak, P.O. Rivera, and I responded to 70 Cedar Avenue on a report of a fight involving a stick. Upon arrival, I spoke with the victim, who is now referred to as Molano-Rivera, Alejandro. He reported that the suspect, who is now referred to as Amadiz, Freddy Jr, assaulted him multiple times to his hands and one time to his head. Alejandro reported that he was walking down his steps, opened the door, and observed Amadiz standing there. Amadiz then squatted down, picked up a stick, and started to assault Alejandro with it multiple times. Alejandro then ran back up the steps and began to scream for help. Amadiz then followed Alejandro up the stairs and continued to assault Alejandro. Alejandro made contact with his father, who was inside the apartment,when the father opened the apartment door, Amadiz took off down the steps and went toward the front of the house. Alejandro reported that Amandiz did not state anything to him during the assault. Alejandro refused medical attention on scene. While Amadiz was assaulting Alejandro, Amandiz caused damage to the ceiling causing a hole. The estimated total cost of repairing the hole in the wall is $300.00.       Sgt. Ghirardi was advised and responded to the scene to assist.      Amandiz entered the listed vehicle and left the scene after the assault occurred with the weapon he used.       Masshiel Barriows, who is the witness, reported hearing the screaming from inside her apartment which is located on the first floor. She reported that when she heard the scream, she went outside and only observed the suspect walking out of the stairs with the stick in his hands, and did not observe the assault occur.    Det/Sgt. Tripodi was advised and responded to the scene to assist and captured pictures of the incident.      Victim Notification forms were completed and were given to the victim.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Tuesday May 13, 2025, I was assigned as the Hackensack High School SRO.   I was notified by the school counselor, Kara Ferrazano, that J.N. disclosed to her that she is involved in an intimate relationship with a 22 year old Hispanic male.       I spoke to the victim in the presence of her case worker, Carolina Valera. The victim confirmed that she is 15 year old and is currently sexually active with her 22 year old boyfriend. She added that they had sex on Sunday May 11, 2025 in his apartment located in the State of New York. The victim stated that this sexual interaction was consensual and refused to provide the identity of the suspect. She also refused to give and exact location of the incident.  I advised her that under New Jersey State Law she could not legally give consent. Various attempts were made to get further information, but the victim refused to cooperate.      I spoke to the victim's mother Maria Zhumi Peralta and advised her of the incident. She stated her daughter had disclosed this incident to her on May 12, 2025. Zhumi Peralta stated she does not know any information about the actor, but will try to obtain it from her daughter.     DCP&P was notified by Mrs. Ferrazano. I notified the Bergen County Prosecutor's Office SVU. I spoke to Det. Negron who advised me that due to the incident occurring in New York I should notify the proper jurisdiction upon obtaining the location of the incident.Case Active pending victim cooperation.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Wednesday, 05/14/2025, I responded to 520 Hackensack Avenue (All American Ford) for a motor vehicle theft. I met with Mr. Nelson Minier (Complainant), who is the general manager at All American Ford. He reported that Ms. Pegeen Callagy was the lease owner of a black 2022 Ford Bronco. Fusion Auto Finance (victim) owns the rights to the vehicle and made arrangements to pick the vehicle up with a third party towing company. The vehicle was parked and secured on the north side of the Ford lot.      The complainant reported that he has been in contact via e-mail with Mr. Sam Carter, who is the manager at Fusion Auto Finance. After arrangements were confirmed between both of them, Richie's Towing responded on 05/05/2025 at 1747 hours for the vehicle and took possession of the vehicle. Mr. Erol Hasan was the tow truck driver that day. Prior to taking the vehicle, he confirmed the vehicle's identification number and he was given a key fob by an employee at Ford. The complainant further reported that the victim contacted him later reporting the vehicle was never delivered.      Today, Mr. Hasan and Mr. Richard Cazassa (Owner of Richie's Towing) responded to Ford after being notified about the vehicle's whereabouts. Mr. Cazassa reported that he has been doing business with Noriega Towing for some time. He further reported that "Danny Noriega" contacted him about subletting a contract for him to pick the 2022 Bronco. Mr. Cazassa sent Mr. Hasan and the vehicle was towed back to his tow yard. Mr. Cazassa reported that he held the vehicle for an unusual amount of time (4 hours) and then "Danny" sent him a drop off location at JC Penny parking lot at Cherry Hill Mall. Mr. Hasan reported that he gave the vehicle to two (2) young black males and in return he was given $1,000.00 in cash for his services. The total value of the vehicle is approximately $36,000.00. Sgt. Det. Tripodi was notified. To be noted, the complainant reported that Ms. Pegeen Callagy received a suspicious phone call from a towing company wanting to pick up the vehicle from her house, in which she denied.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: I responded to police headquarters on a report of a burglary to a residence.  Upon arrival I made contact with the victim, Vanessa Durango, who stated initially that she believes her ex-boyfriend, Max Makarov (suspect), stole money from her.  The victim stated that on Monday, May 12, 2025 at approximately 0800 hours, the suspect got home and asked the victim for some money and food.  The victim stated that she did not have any money but he can help himself to some food and then went back to sleep.  The victim then woke back up at 1130 hours and realized $500.00 was missing from her purse.  The victim tried contacting the suspect via phone with negative results.       Furthermore, the victim stated that she got home this afternoon as approximately 1200 hours and saw her apartment was ransacked.  The victim is unaware if the suspect has copies of her keys and did not notice any visible forced entry to the home.  The victim did not give permission to the suspect to enter her home.  The victim stated that she noticed her yellow Gucci purse was missing that has an estimated cost of $1000.00.  The victim also stated that she had money taken out of her Capital One Bank Account totaling $2200.00.  She was given a fraud packet to fill out and advised to bring back with all proper documentation and we would create a supplement report.  The victim was given a copy of the victim notification form.  Detective Dogali was advised of the incident.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: I responded to the listed location on a reported criminal mischief. On arrival, Sgt. Farhi was on scene speaking with the victims. Samora Turner (Victim), the resident, reported that while she was inside her apartment the suspect came inside her home without permission and entered on the Briscolina Street entrance of her apartment that was unlocked at the time. It should be noted that the suspect is known to both victims as a friend. An argument ensued between the victims and the suspect, as the suspect attempted to punch Samora Turner with closed fist, her friend (Alexis Salas) which was inside the apartment also stepped in front of her and the suspect punched her in the face. The suspect then took Alexis Salas's (Victim) Iphone 15 pro valued at $1,200,00 USD and exited via the front door of the apartment which is located on the Lodi Street side. The suspect then attempted to re enter the apartment but was locked out and subsequently kicked the door in causing damage to the frame of the door of about $500.00 USD. The argument continued until the suspect fled eastbound on Lodi Street , stopped and slammed the Iphone 15 pro on the ground causing it to shatter. The incident was  witnessed by S.T., as she was inside the apartment the entire time.      The victim, Alexis Salas complained of pain to her right eye but refused medical attention. Both victims refused to complete a voluntary hand written statement. They both refused to respond to police headquarters to provide a recorded statement but were advised to respond at a later time if they wished. Victim notification forms were completed for both victims and they received copies.      A handle with care form was completed for S.T. Det. Tanucilli was notified and advised of the incident.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On 05/25/25 P.O. W. Rivera, P.O. I. Papanikolaou and I were dispatched to 20 Prospect, Suite 602, to a suspected burglary in the past. Upon arrival to the listed location, I met with Ms. Alfaro, who is the Operations Manager. I then met Ms. Guzman who is a Palliative Care Coordinator, and Mr. Bravo, who is a nurse at the practice. Ms. Alfaro stated that Mr. Bravo and Ms. Guzman, upon arriving to work at 0700 hrs, witnessed the suspect sitting in the front desk area. Mr. Bravo believed he locked the office doors when he left work yesterday, 05/24/25, at approximately 1900 hrs. Ms. Guzman tried talking to the suspect to ask why she was there, and the suspect did not respond. Ms. Guzman stated that the suspect was mumbling to herself incoherently, and did not make eye contact. The suspect then moved toward the door. The suspect then placed a pair of glasses down on the table next to the front door, and walked out of the office. It is unknown if the glasses belonged to the suspect or a staff member.After the suspect left the office, Mr. Bravo and Ms. Guzman stated they checked all the rooms to see if anything was missing. Upon checking the rooms, they observed the suspect had made multiple stacks of coins on the desk in one of the rooms. Its unknown if the coins belong to a staff member, or if they belong to the suspect. They also observed that the suspect had eaten food in their office break room. She left behind a drink, a half eaten chocolate bar, and other remnants of food on the break room table.  They also found that the suspect left a sticky note with a number 973-223-5491 and the name Great Darris Grandpa on the break room table. Mr. Bravo and Ms. Guzman stated no items of value appear to be missing at this time. Ms. Guzman then called Ms. Alfaro, and Ms. Alfaro called security. Ms. Alfaro afterward contacted Hackensack PD. Sgt. Butler arrived on scene and called the number on the sticky note. Sgt. Butler was able to make contact with the party listed on the note. The contact told Sgt.  Butler that the suspect's name was Tammi Brodish. The contact informed Sgt. Butler that the suspect suffers from mental illness. The contact is unaware of the suspect's current whereabouts, but believes she maybe homeless. HUMC security viewed the cameras and gave the description of the suspect. Suspect was described as a white female, wearing a grey hoodie and white leopard print pants. The cameras showed she entered the office at approximately 0600 hrs and left the office at approximately 0710 hrs. Suspect was then observed heading toward the service elevator.Sgt. Butler notified Lt. Levis who Det. Sgt. Tripodi. The scene had been disturbed by employees prior to police arrival.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect Descriptions: Five Hispanic/Dominican males, unknown clothing description. Seen fleeing the area in two vehicles, one possibly a white Honda sedan.     P.O. He further stated that the door was slightly ajar and that one of the suspects suddenly kicked it, causing it to fully open. The victim reported that the five suspects entered his home, with one of them stating that they were "looking for someone". The victim advised that his primary language is Spanish and that the suspects spoke to him in English. He further reported that he had asked the suspects to leave, at which time the main suspect told him "Don't talk to me like that". The victim stated that the suspects then left the residence, with him guiding them out the front door. He further stated that one of the suspects purposely struck the victim with the screen door as they were leaving, causing him slight pain to his arm. When asked if he needed medical attention, he declined.    Sgt. Farhi was contacted and advised of the circumstances. The victim was advised that detectives would be in contact with him in the following days due to the victim touching multiple surfaces prior to our arrival, the scene was unable to be processed. A Victim Notification Form was completed and provided to the victim. The victim did not wish to completely a voluntary hand written statement at the time of this report.   While canvassing the area, I observed exterior cameras on 100 Troast and 79 Troast, which may have captured the incident.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Sunday, May 25, 2025 at approximately 19:23 hours, I responded to the lobby of 60 Kansas Street on a report of a theft. Upon my arrival, I met with Ms. Shannon Gaddy (Complainant) who wished to report several missing items from her deceased brother's apartment. The complainant advised me, that she had received an e-mail from the Housing Authority to clean out her deceased brother's (Norman Gaddy) apartment by June 1, 2025. The complainant further advised me, that she came to the apartment today at approximately 19:00 hours, and began removing her brother's belongings when she noticed several items belonging to her brother missing. At that point, I asked the complainant if she knew of anyone other than herself that had access to the apartment to which she stated "No". The complainant then stated that the last time she was in her brother's apartment was the day he passed which was on April 26, 2025 and since then, no one to her knowledge had entered the apartment.  As per the complainant, no other friend or family member had key access to the apartment other than her. The complainant stated, that the only other individuals with key access to the apartment were the building's maintenance department. I checked the exterior door to the apartment and it appeared to be secure and did not exhibit any signs of forced entry. I also checked the hallway for cameras and there were no cameras located anywhere in the hallway or corridor of the second floor or outside of the apartment. Due to the complainant's observations and statements, I believed a possible burglary had occurred and contacted headquarters and requested for a supervisor to respond. Shortly thereafter, Sgt. Farhi arrived on scene and was advised of the circumstances. After speaking with myself and the complainant, Sgt. Farhi concurred that a burglary may have occurred and contacted the on-call Detective. Sgt. Farhi spoke with Detective Lara-Nunez and it was determined that Detective Lara-Nunez would conduct a follow up investigation at a later date and time. Due to the complainant already entering the apartment and moving and touching items in the apartment, the Bergen County Sheriff's Office Bureau of Criminal Identification was not contacted. The list of belongings removed from the apartment are as follows: 1. (1)  Sony Playstation 7 valued at approximately $750.00 dollars. 2. (1) Poland Spring Water Jug containing a $20 dollar bill and a large quantity of coins valued at approximately $350 dollars.  3. (1) Pair of silver studded earings valued at approximately $60.00 dollars. 4. (1) Silver chain and cross valued at approximately $100.00 dollars.Total value of missing property is $1,260 dollars. The complainant was provided with a victim notification form and advised that she would be receiving a follow up call in a few days from the Detective Bureau.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On 05/26/25, Sgt. Farhi, Sgt. Tedesco, P.O. Sroka, P.O. Almonte, and I responded to 505 S. River Street on a report of an unarmed fight. Upon arrival, I met with victim John Rannella, who stated a white sedan BMW, a green sedan BMW, and a blue Honda or Toyota (unknown license plates at this time) were blocking other vehicles in the parking lot of the listed location. Rannella approached the group and requested them to move their vehicles. The three vehicles exited the parking lot of the listed location.     At approximately 23:27 hours, the white BMW parked at the intersection of S. River Street and E. Moonachie Road. The Green BMW parked in front of 8 E. Moonachie Road. Rannella walked up to the intersection of S. River Street and E. Moonachie Road. The drivers and passengers exited their vehicle and began to assault Rannella by throwing punches and throwing a full 'Essentia' water bottle to his face. Rannella had lacerations to his face/nose area. Victim William Burke approached the suspects as Rannella was being assaulted. The suspects then assaulted Burke by throwing a punch to his face. Burke stated he fell to the ground.  As he was attempting to get up on his feet his phone fell out of his pocket. The suspects then grabbed Burke's phone, entered their vehicle, then fled the area.     Hackensack Volunteer EMS #1061 responded to assist. Both victims, Rannella and Burke, refused further medical attention.      Det. D. Carroll was notified and responded. Burke was transported to police headquarters for statements in the rear seat of radio car 121. The rear seat was checked prior and after transport with negative results.      A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed. A copy was explained and given to the victim.      Surveillance video is available at a later date.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On June 3rd, 2025, I responded to 160 Overlook Avenue on a report of a motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, the victim stated that his motorcycle, bearing NJ Reg 4HXZ4, was stolen from its parking space. The victim stated that he secured his motorcycle in a covered parking garage at the listed address, parking level G3, space #51. The victim reported last observing it on approximately 5/1/25. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx

Nursing staff notified the Bergen County Prosecutor's Office Special Victim's Unit. Detective Atie from the Special Victim's Unit was apprised of the incident. According to the victim, the incident occurred outside of 167 Central Avenue on 6/1/2025. The exact time and location of the incident is unconfirmed. The suspect's identify is also unknown. A Sexual Assault Response Team nurse was dispatched for the examination. The examination was conducted and completed at the hospital.    On 6/4/2025, I responded to the Bergen County Prosecutor's Office Special Victim's Unit to assist Detective Atie with the victim's interview. The victim was scheduled for an interview at 1100 hours. The victim did not arrive for the interview as scheduled. At approximately 1200 hours, Detective Atie attempted to contact the victim by phone with negative results. For further details surrounding the incident, refer to Detective Atie's report  (BCPO : 25-00708).    **This case will remain Active/Administratively Closed pending cooperation from the victim***

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Thursday, June 5th, 2025 at 0704 hours, I responded to 411 Simons Avenue on a burglary to auto. Upon arrival, I met with the complainant who stated he observed three (3) males dressed in black and wearing black masks surrounding the victim's vehicle (NJ-P55UNM).  He observed them smash out the rear passenger side window and enter the vehicle. The complainant yelled at the suspects which caused them to flee.  They fled in a white sedan that had both license plates covered.  The vehicle fled East on Simons Avenue towards South Summit Avenue.     Sgt. Tovbin responded to assist. The registered owner, Denise Nunez was unable to respond.  The registered owner's son, John Nunez, responded. He is the primary user of her vehicle. We observed the rear passenger window completely smashed out with shattered glass in and out of the car. Without touching the vehicle, John Nunez was able to determine that nothing was stolen from his mother's vehicle. The registered owner and John Nunez denied having the vehicle processed for fingerprints.    John Nunez captured the entire incident on a camera affixed to 416 Simons Avenue.  He saved the footage and will send it to the Hackensack Detective's e-mail. The video confirmed everything the complainant reported. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect description:  Black female, skinny build, approximately 5' 5" tall, medium brown complexion, and she was last seen wearing a light blue scarf wrapped around her head, blue jean jacket, and unknown color pants.  She was also holding a gray laptop that was open with light blue headphone on her head.  She also has a black bookbag with her.       On June 8th, 2025, I responded to 111 River Street, TD Bank, on a report of a dispute between two parties.  Upon arrival, I met with Carlos Argueta, the Victim, and he stated that he was withdrawing some money from the ATM machine prior to returning home, 160 River Street Apt. #262, Hackensack, NJ 07601.        When he initially entered the ATM vestibule, there was an unknown black female, the Suspect, already in the vestibule, sitting down on the floor.  He withdrew his money, turned around and the Suspect began speaking to the Victim.  The Victim stated that the Suspect took out a long object that appeared to be a bat out of a black bag.  She then swung it in the air and stated to the Victim that she was going to "fuck him up." The Suspect then pushed the Victim around the waist area and took $40.00 (Two (2) twenty dollar bills) that was in his right hand. Donnerstag also responded to assist.  The Victim stated that he will provide a statement to Detectives and will proceed with a criminal complaint if the Suspect was located and identified.  He also stated that he would be able to identify the Suspect or the Suspect's photograph in the future.  Det. Mora was notified and responded to obtain a statement from the Victim.  See Det. Mora's Supplemental Report for further information.       At Headquarters, the Victim mentioned that the Suspect stated something about the Victim's sexuality and being gay. The Victim was provided with a copy of the Victim Notification Form.  It was also confirmed that the Victim did not sustain any injuries and refused any medical assistance.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: Suspect description: Black male, approximately 30 years old, wearing black hooded sweatshirt, black sweatpants, and black sneakers.   On 6/11/25 I responded to 150 Main Street, on a reported motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I made contact with the victim / Amazon driver, Andryk Inojosavalor, who did not speak English. I requested for a Spanish speaking officer. P.O. W Rivera and P.O. Aberde responded. The victim stated he parked his vehicle (bearing NJ Reg V82UPJ) in the parking space right in front of 150 Main Street. The victim further stated on 6/11/25 at approximately 04:31 hours he exited his vehicle. Before the victim entered 150 Main Street to deliver a package, he observed only one person walking toward the direction of his vehicle. The victim reported the vehicle was left on and unlocked when he entered the building. The key to the vehicle was also left in the vehicle. The victim further reported upon arriving back to his vehicle at approximately 04:32 hours he witnessed the suspect drive off with his vehicle going east on Mercer Street. The estimated value of the vehicle is $8,500.00 USD according to the victim.   Headquarters was notified. The vehicle details was put out on a SPEN message.   I then spoke to the concierge, Timothy Rayford, who was not able to provide the video surveillance of the suspect at this time. Mr. Rayford advised he was going to notify his manager and that he would be able to provide me with the video surveillance at a later time. The victim later reported the following items were inside the vehicle at the time of the incident:- Wallet (estimated value is $30.00).- $20.00 USD in the wallet.- 2 License Plates (estimated value is $20.00).- 1 Bank of America Debit Card.- 1 Bank of America Credit Card.- 1 Capital One Debit Card.- 1 Capital One Credit Card.- 1 BJ's Membership Card.- 1 Mercantile Credit Card.- Multiple Amazon Packages that were not delivered yet. The total estimated value of items stolen is $8,570.00.   A NJ Victim Notification Form was completed and a copy was provided to the victim. The stolen vehicle was entered into Auto Theft Task Force and NICIC. A Motor Vehicle Theft Investigation Checklist was completed and submitted to the Detective's Bureau. Sgt. Butler was advised.      A consent to search form was not signed on scene. However, the victim was contacted after the fact and stated that he was unable to respond to headquarters to at this time to since he did not have transportation. Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: I responded to the listed location on a reported burglary to a residence. Upon arrival, I spoke with the victim and her family. She further stated that her husband and daughter were sleeping in another room while she was gone. The victim reported that she returned to the rear of her home at 10:00 hours. She further reported that when she re-entered her home, she observed the kitchen to be in disarray, with chairs knocked over and cleaning products knocked onto the ground. The victim stated that she then alerted her family, who reported the mess was not their doing. She further stated that they noticed their family cat was missing and then attempted to locate her, proving unsuccessful.    I then requested a supervisor. Sgt's Tedesco and Farhi responded to assist. Det. Sgt. Williams and Det. Dogali also responded and were advised.      The victim reported that no items, valuables or furniture was missing and the only thing taken was the family cat.     The cat, Ebony, is described as a black and white Tuxexdo cat, 3 years old with a pink collar around her neck containing contact information, with no health issues.     Due to the victim touching multiple surfaces prior to our arrival, the scene was unable to be processed. A Victim Notification Form was completed and provided to the victim. The victim did not wish to completely a voluntary hand written statement at the time of this report.   While canvassing the area, I observed exterior cameras on 263 Park Street and 252 Park Street, which may have captured the incident.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On June 21, 2025, at approximately 09:26 hours, I responded in marked radio car #117 to 274 State Street (city parking lot - Area D), for a report of a burglary. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who stated that someone tried to steal his vehicle.     The victim stated that he parked the listed vehicle (NJ/M60JZJ) directly behind 251 Main Street, in a marked parking space (space #20) facing east, at approximately 23:30 hours on the date of 06/20/2025.  He returned to his vehicle on this date at approximately 09:00 hours and noticed that the front passenger side door was left ajar and the rear driver side window was broken.  The victim also noticed that the compartment under the steering wheel was left open and the ignition to the vehicle was tampered with.  The victim stated that the vehicle is no longer functional due to the incident. The victim reported that none of his belongings were missing from the vehicle.  The estimated cost of repairs is approximately $2000.00.     Due to the tampering of the ignition, it appeared that the incident was an attempt to steal the vehicle.  The victim stated that the vehicle has an anti theft system installed.  I observed pry marks on the window sill which led me to believe that burglary tools may have been utilized to gain entry.  Due to the vehicle being contaminated by the victim, the scene was unable to be processed for fingerprints.  There are several cameras in the area that may have captured the incident.  The closest camera to the incident is located on the rear of 270 State Street.  Sgt. Gretkowski was notified of the incident.     A New Jersey Victim Notification Form was completed and a copy was given to the victim.  The victim wishes to be notified if any parties involved can be identified.Vehicle Insurance: Company Code 017, Policy #HPA00002847687.

Name: 2025_07_08_10_40_55_ALL_RMS.xlsx
Narrative: On Monday, July 7, 2025, at approximately 14:40 hours, P.O. Small, P.O. Han, P.O. J. Peralta, and I responded to 65 Court Street (Pep Boys) on a report of a robbery. Sgt. Donnerstag also responded. Upon arrival, P.O. Small was interviewing the suspects while I spoke with the victim, Shomari Mighty. MIGHTY reported that while walking on the river walk in the rear of the listed location, the arrestee, Jose Colon-Nunez, confronted him. MIGHTY further reported, COLON-NUNEZ  was accompanied by the two suspects, Eliandry Guerrero and Oswaldo Perez. MIGHTY indicated, COLON-NUNEZ mistook him for someone who assaulted him the day prior. See case 25-056199 for further information regarding the aforementioned assault.      MIGHTY added that when he denied assaulting COLON-NUNEZ, GUERRERO took his "off brand Samsung" smart phone from his hand and threw it in the river. MIGHTY stated that the phone is valued at $36.00.  MIGHTY further reported that after GUERRERO threw his phone, COLON-NUNEZ struck him in the left leg with a black baseball bat. MIGHTY further reported that after being struck with the bat, PEREZ struck him on the right hip with a small black garden shovel. Both shovel and baseball bat were located near the river walk and logged into evidence. MIGHTY reported that following the assault with the bat and shovel, GUERRERO utilized a can of pepper spray to spray him in the neck, arm, and chest area. MIGHTY complained of pain to his left leg, right hip, and left arm. MIGHTY requested medical assistance and soon after, Hackensack Meridian Health ambulance 1350 arrived on scene. MIGHTY agreed to provide a recorded statement of the incident. However, he was transported Hackensack University Medical Center for evaluation.     I then spoke with GUERRERO, PEREZ, and COLON-NUNEZ.
